message:
feat(rchd): surface cancellation health issues in daemon status API (bd-1yt6)

Add cancellation-aware issue generation to the /status API endpoint so
operators and automated tooling (doctor, TUI) receive actionable
diagnostics about recent cancellation events without manually
inspecting daemon logs.

New function cancellation_issues_from_recent_builds() scans the 20
most recent build records for cancellation metadata and emits
structured Issue entries:

- severity=error when any cancellation ended with cleanup_ok=false,
  indicating slots, workdirs, or history may be in inconsistent state.
  Remediation directs operators to probe workers and inspect
  cancellation_failed events.

- severity=warning when cancellations escalated to SIGKILL, meaning
  graceful termination failed and force-kill was required. Suggests
  checking for stuck toolchain phases via `rch status --jobs`.

- severity=warning when cancellations completed while the worker was
  unreachable, indicating potential connectivity or fleet health
  issues. Directs operators to `rch workers probe --all`.

- severity=info when all recent cancellations completed cleanly with
  deterministic cleanup, confirming the cancellation subsystem is
  healthy.

The handle_status() function now calls this after collecting recent
builds, so the issues array in the status response automatically
includes cancellation health signals alongside existing disk pressure,
circuit breaker, and convergence issues.

Also reformats the handle_cancel_build() call site for line length
compliance (rustfmt).

New test: test_handle_status_emits_cancellation_cleanup_issue creates
an active build, cancels it with metadata containing cleanup_ok=false,
sigkill escalation, and unreachable worker health, then verifies the
status response includes both "cleanup failures" and "SIGKILL" issue
summaries.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
